L1.area: Product catalog management and record editing functionality

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied HTTP request parameters are directly used in database queries without proper type sanitization, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands via crafted form input values.

L3.area: Product record creation and update form processing within the product management module
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where HTTP request parameters controlling product attributes such as batch management status are directly interpolated into database operations without type filtering or sanitization, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands via crafted product form submissions.

ground_truth.reason: The status_batch parameter and other product form fields in the product editing page are taken directly from unsanitized GET/POST input and used in database queries, allowing remote SQL injection.
